name: Threat Coverage (Malware/Ransomware)
description: Effectiveness in detecting and blocking commodity and advanced malware/ransomware strains.

rubric.1: Legacy / Signature-based only. < 70% detection rate against modern ransomware.
rubric.2: Basic / Signature + partial heuristics. Misses polymorphic threats.
rubric.3: Competent / 90%+ detection of known threats. Some behavioral analysis.
rubric.4: Advanced / 99% detection. Robust behavioral blocking & ransomware rollback.
rubric.5: World-Class / 100% efficacy in major tests (MITRE/AV-TEST). Zero-delay blocking.

name: Zero-Day Detection
description: Capability to identify unknown threats using advanced AI/ML and behavioral heuristics without prior signatures.

rubric.1: None / Relies entirely on known signatures/hashes.
rubric.2: Weak / High false positive rate on unknown files. Sandbox took > 10 mins.
rubric.3: Moderate / Detects common unknown packers/variants. ~5 min verdict.
rubric.4: Strong / AI-driven pre-execution prevention. < 2 min verdict for unknowns.
rubric.5: Leading / Real-time (< 30s) blocking of never-before-seen payloads via deep learning.

name: Phishing & Email Security
description: Integration and efficacy in detecting malicious emails, BEC, and malicious links/attachments.

rubric.1: None / No email integration or reliance on external gateway only.
rubric.3: Standard / NLP-based checking for body text. Attachment sandboxing.
rubric.4: Advanced / API-level inbox remediation. Computer vision for credential harvesting pages.
rubric.5: Comprehensive / Integrated user training, auto-clawback, and social graph analysis.

name: False Positive Rate
description: Accuracy of alerts to minimize alert fatigue.

rubric.1: Poor / > 20% of alerts are false positives. Unusable for automation.
rubric.2: Below Average / 10-20% FP rate. Constant tuning required.
rubric.3: Average / 5-10% FP rate. Manageable with a dedicated analyst.
rubric.4: Good / < 5% FP rate. High confidence in critical alerts.
rubric.5: Excellent / < 1% FP rate. Alerts are actionable by default.

name: Detection Latency
description: Time elapsed between the malicious activity and the alert generation.

rubric.1: Laggy / > 1 hour delay for critical alerts.
rubric.2: Slow / 15-60 minutes delay. Batch processing evident.
rubric.3: Standard / 1-15 minutes delay. Near real-time for some logs.
rubric.4: Fast / < 1 minute for local events. < 5 mins for cloud logs.
rubric.5: Real-time / Sub-second detection and alerting pipeline.

name: MITRE ATT&CK Mapping
description: Depth and accuracy of alert mapping to MITRE TTPs.

rubric.1: None / Alerts have no framework context.
rubric.2: Superficial / Maps to high-level tactics only (e.g., 'Execution').
rubric.3: Standard / Maps to technique IDs (T1xxx) but lacks sub-technique detail.
rubric.4: Detailed / Maps to sub-techniques. Includes prevention guidance.
rubric.5: Actionable / Dynamic mapping with coverage heatmaps and gap analysis.

name: Cloud Workload Protection
description: Detection capabilities specific to containers, serverless, and cloud VMs.

rubric.1: None / Agent does not support cloud environments.
rubric.2: Basic / Traditional agent installed on cloud VMs only.
rubric.3: Moderate / K8s node visibility. AWS flows.
rubric.4: Advanced / DaemonSets for K8s, Fargate support, API scanning.
rubric.5: Native / Agentless scanning + Runtime protection across multi-cloud.

name: SOAR / Automation Capabilities
description: Native capabilities to orchestrate workflows and automate responses.

rubric.1: Manual / No automation. Analyst must manually perform every step.
rubric.3: Partial / Pre-built playbooks for common tasks (e.g., block IP, isolate host).
rubric.4: Extensive / Visual playbook editor, multi-step branching logic, approval workflows.
rubric.5: Autonomous / AI-recommended actions and fully self-healing workflows for Tier 1 incidents.

name: Playbook Ecosystem
description: Availability and quality of out-of-the-box response playbooks.

rubric.3: Standard / 20-50 common scenario playbooks (phishing, malware).
rubric.4: Rich / 100+ verified partner integrations and playbooks.
rubric.5: Community-Driven / Marketplace with thousands of community/vendor maintained playbooks.

name: Remediation Depth
description: Granularity of response actions available on endpoints/assets.

rubric.3: Basic / IP blocking, Process kill, Hash ban.
rubric.4: Granular / Delete file, registry edit, service restart, user disable.
rubric.5: Surgical / Live shell access, memory dump, script injection, granular firewall control.

name: Investigation Workbench
description: Quality of the interface for analysts to triage and investigate.

rubric.1: Disjointed / Multiple browser tabs needed. Raw log views only.
rubric.2: Basic / Simple aggregation of alerts. No correlation visualizer.
rubric.3: Functional / Timeline view, pivoted searches, basic correlation.
rubric.4: Streamlined / Graph view of assets/threats, integrated threat intel lookup.
rubric.5: Integrated / Unified workspace. One-click pivoting, auto-enriched artifacts, collaboration tools.

name: Ticketing Integration
description: Bi-directional sync with ITSM tools (Jira, ServiceNow).

rubric.2: Uni-directional / Can send emails/webhooks to open tickets.
rubric.3: Basic Sync / Opens tickets via API. No status updates back.
rubric.4: Bi-directional / Status/Comments sync both ways.
rubric.5: Native App / Deep app integration with custom fields and workflow mapping.

name: API Completeness
description: Extent to which platform features are accessible via API.

rubric.2: Limited / Read-only API or limited endpoints.
rubric.3: Standard / Most data queryable. Some configuration gaps.
rubric.4: Robust / comprehensive REST/GraphQL API for data & config.
rubric.5: API-First / 100% feature parity. SDKs available in Python/Go/JS.

name: Data Ingestion Flexibility
description: Ability to parse and normalize logs from custom/diverse sources.

rubric.2: Standard / Supports Syslog/CEF/LEEF. Hard to parse custom apps.
rubric.3: Flexible / JSON/XML parsing. Regex-based custom parsers.
rubric.4: Universal / Smart connectors, auto-schema detection, Logstash/Fluentd support.
rubric.5: Schemaless / Ingest anything. Search-time extraction. Zero parsing overhead.

name: Ecosystem Integrations
description: Breadth of pre-built technology partner integrations.

rubric.3: Average / 50-100 integrations (Major Cloud, FW, EDR).
rubric.4: Extensive / 300+ seamless integrations.
rubric.5: Universal / 500+ integrations + Open Collector framework.

name: Custom Detection Logic
description: Language and flexibility for writing custom detection rules.

rubric.3: Intermediate / Proprietary query language (e.g., KQL, SPL).
rubric.4: Advanced / SIGMA rule support, Yara support.
rubric.5: Code-Level / Python/Lua scripting for complex stateful logic.

name: Multi-Tenancy
description: Support for MSSP/Hierarchical data separation.

rubric.2: Weak / Logical separation but shared RBAC.
rubric.3: Functional / Separate data silos, shared config.
rubric.4: Robust / Full RBAC, varied retention per tenant, cross-tenant hunting.
rubric.5: Carrier-Grade / physical separation options, white-labeling, infinite nesting.

name: Ingestion Throughput
description: Events Per Second (EPS) capability without lag.

rubric.3: High / 5k-20k EPS. Enterprise standard.
rubric.4: Very High / 20k-100k EPS. Handles high-volume log sources (DNS/Flow).
rubric.5: Hyperscale / > 100k EPS. Horizontal auto-scaling.

name: Search Speed (Hot Data)
description: Query response time for last 24 hours of data.

rubric.1: Slow / Minutes to return simple queries.
rubric.3: Fast / < 5 seconds for indexed fields.
rubric.4: Instant / Sub-second for most queries.
rubric.5: Real-time / Instant results with live-tail capabilities.

name: Historical Search (Cold Data)
description: Ability and speed to search archived/cold logs (30d+).

rubric.2: Painful / Hours to run queries over long timeframes.
rubric.3: Workable / Slower than hot, but runs in background without timeouts.
rubric.4: Seamless / Search hot & cold transparently (Tiered storage).
rubric.5: Instant / innovative format allowing sub-second capability on years of data.

name: Agent Resource Footprint
description: CPU/RAM impact on endpoints.

rubric.1: Heavy / Noticeable slowdown (> 5% CPU). User complaints.
rubric.2: Moderate / spikes during scanning, steady otherwise.
rubric.3: Light / < 2% CPU average. < 200MB RAM.
rubric.4: Very Light / < 1% CPU. < 100MB RAM.
rubric.5: Invisible / Kernel-level efficiency. Negligible impact.

name: Scalability Architecture
description: Ease of expanding capacity.

rubric.3: Clustered / Add nodes manually.
rubric.4: Elastic / Auto-scaling groups (Cloud).
rubric.5: Serverless / Fully managed SaaS. No infrastructure management.

name: Deployment Time-to-Value
description: Effort required to go from purchase to actionable insights.

rubric.3: Days / Standard cloud deployment.
rubric.4: Hours / SaaS signup + Agent deploy.
rubric.5: Minutes / Zero-touch, auto-discovery.

name: User Interface / UX
description: Intuitiveness and aesthetic quality of the console.

rubric.2: Cluttered / Information overload, hard to navigate.
rubric.3: Functional / Standard dashboards. Learning curve exists.
rubric.4: Modern / Clean, responsive, dark mode, intuitive workflows.
rubric.5: Consumer-Grade / Delightful, gamified, contextual help, fully customizable.

name: Documentation & Learning
description: Quality of self-help resources.

rubric.3: Good / Searchable knowledge base, decent coverage.
rubric.4: Excellent / Videos, interactive labs, active community.
rubric.5: World-Class / Integrated academy, certification paths, in-product contextual guides.

name: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
description: Granularity of user permissions.

rubric.3: Standard / Custom roles, AD/LDAP integration.
rubric.4: Granular / Field-level data masking, index-level access.
rubric.5: Attribute-Based / Dynamic policy-based access control (ABAC).

name: Compliance Reporting
description: Coverage of standard frameworks (PCI, HIPAA, GDPR, SOC2).

rubric.2: Skeletal / Generic reports requiring heavy modification.
rubric.3: Standard / PDF packs for major regulations.
rubric.4: Interactive / Drill-down compliance dashboards.
rubric.5: Continuous / Real-time GRC monitoring and mapping.

name: Custom Reporting Engine
description: Flexibility in creating new reports.

rubric.2: Limited / Basic filters on existing templates.
rubric.3: Visual / Drag-and-drop widget builder.
rubric.4: Code-Based / SQL/BI tool integration.
rubric.5: Limitless / Pixel-perfect embedded BI (e.g., Looker/Tableau integration).

name: Data Retention Flexibility
description: Options for storing logs for compliance.

rubric.3: Configurable / Per-index settings.
rubric.4: Hybrid / Store locally or bring-your-own-storage (S3).
rubric.5: Unlimited / Economical long-term cold storage inclusions.

name: Pricing Predictability
description: Transparency and stability of cost.

rubric.2: Complex / Mix of events, users, and module add-ons.
rubric.3: Moderate / Node/Endpoint based. Predictable but expensive scale.
rubric.4: Transparent / Flat fee or simplistic user/device bands.
rubric.5: Value-Based / Unlimited data options. Pay for capability, not volume.

name: Total Cost of Ownership
description: Holistic cost including infrastructure, admin, and license.

rubric.1: Very High / Expensive license + heavy hardware + dedicated staff.
rubric.2: High / Moderate license but high maintenance.
rubric.3: Average / Market standard.
rubric.4: Low / SaaS efficiencies reduce admin cost.
rubric.5: Minimal / High ROI. Displaces other tools to lower net spend.

name: Vendor Support Quality
description: Support SLA and technical competence.

rubric.3: Standard / 24/7 Phone, tiered SLA.
rubric.4: Premium / Dedicated Technical Account Manager (TAM).
rubric.5: Partner / Engineering-level access, proactive health checks.

name: Threat Intelligence Quality
description: Quality of the native threat intel feed.

rubric.3: Commercial / Standard vendor feed.
rubric.4: Premium / Top-tier unit (e.g., Unit42, Mandiant, Talos).
rubric.5: Bespoke / Industry-specific intel + dark web monitoring.

name: AI/ML Efficacy
description: Effectiveness of AI features in noise reduction and detection.

rubric.3: Helpful / User Baseline Analytics (UEBA).
rubric.4: Advanced / Automatic incident correlation, root cause analysis.
rubric.5: Transformational / Generative AI Copilot (LLM) for querying/reporting/response.

name: Marketplace Apps
description: Availability of third-party apps.

rubric.3: Standard / Dashboards and connectors.
rubric.4: Rich / Full-blown apps (e.g., Vulnerability mgmt module).
rubric.5: Thriving / Developer platform with monetization.

name: Identity Threat Detection
description: Capabilities to detect compromised credentials and lateral movement.

rubric.3: Standard / Impossible travel, new location.
rubric.4: Advanced / Kerberoasting, Golden Ticket detection, LDAP pattern analysis.
rubric.5: Identity-First / Unified ITDR. Real-time MFA prompt bombing detection.

name: Network Traffic Analysis (NTA)
description: Analysis of network flows and PCAP.

rubric.3: Metadata / L7 protocol analysis (DNS/HTTP).
rubric.4: PCAP / Full packet capture on demand.
rubric.5: Deep / Encrypted traffic analysis (JA3), automatic PCAP extraction.

name: Forensic Capabilities
description: Tools for deep-dive investigation.

rubric.3: Basic / File retrieval.
rubric.4: Advanced / Live memory analysis, MFT parsing.
rubric.5: Complete / Full remote forensics suite integrated.

name: Vulnerability Context
description: Integration of vuln data into incident context.

rubric.3: Visual / Vulnerabilities shown on asset view.
rubric.4: Correlated / Alerts prioritized by asset vulnerability status.
rubric.5: Predictive / Attack path modeling based on vulnerabilities.
